item.title: I was wondering what happened to this kid and if he's still cancer free...and he is, 6yrs on from his diagnosis he's doing really well! So thought I'd share some nice info I saw today!!
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: Backlogged memes: f i l t h
item.description:
Not at all an inaccurate summation of LOTR Hello all I am getting rid of all the memes on my phone and if you like them I’ll get more
Catholics have their flaws but their aesthetic SLAPS
My dad is the opposite. I once walked into the kitchen to see him holding up a hand with A MASSIVE NAIL SHOT RIGHT THROUGH IT AND BLEEDING EVERYWHERE while he chatted with his firefighter buddies on the phone like hey yeah man can you send an ambulance so I don’t pass out and leave my daughter all alone thanks Anyway my therapist LOVES that story she wrote down every single detail and asked lots of questions
You can do both. Just not at the same time.
This persons mom gets it
Every other man just ain’t even trying are they
I feel like Home Depot would appreciate that but at Lowe’s they would hang you from the rafters with an electrical cord. Although that’s too much effort.
It’s true they can’t talk about dresses what is that shit
My son stayed in for 42 weeks and they had to drill a thing into his skull to get him out. I bet his first escape room will be him setting up shop and refusing to leave until he’s dragged out kicking and screaming.
What else could it possibly be???
If you EVER get the opportunity to do this, do it. I know it’s not always financially viable but holy SHIT the power you feel
Good friend though. Like if a friend won’t bang you to cheer you up then you need new friends
My husbands cat is like this
The vibrator is an excellent touch. Still on the fence about if it helps or not.
Must be nice… this is like the only app I’ve ever been on for non marketing purposes and that’s only because nobody here gives a shit about my book
She is who I want to be when I grow up
This is like. The best life lesson possible about criticism from others. I want to send this to every author I know.
Ok no no it is not a travel trend although yes it is dangerous on flights. That’s how you get chlamydia and dick bruises
I kind of want them to do a twilight remake but with Anna Kendrick as Bella, the entire plot would make so much more sense.
I did do that a lot. My online friend and my online crush turned out to be the same person tho.
This is how central Texans talk to deer. East Texans are like OMG A DEER HONEY GET THE CAMERA and west Texans just shoot them.
Octopi could totally pull that off.
To be fair it’s not a universally known term. I just learned a new one and then forgot it, they are hard to remember
Pride and prejudice memes >>>>>
Kickstarter for $1 million probably knowing this crazy world
I get more annoyed with him than I do with anyone else and then of course he’s the one I want to vent to smh
May I be the first to say that this format of texting is super annoying, just screenshot the whole thing
Ah, early twenties
It isnt. You could try book nooks though. They’re cute.
And my fav: fight systems, not individuals
Every day I just get more pissed off about this
You literally can’t get plastic straws here in Germany. Not even for kids. Makes it a lot harder to swallow *ba dum tiss*
Not a big believer in plastic surgery but if someone else was paying I think I would turn to sin
Gotta take some you time
Husband went to the part of Scotland that is a puffin sanctuary
Everyone else go home
Es Verdad
It would be soooo much more fun if they WERE gay. All those late nights take on a new meaning hmmmmmm?
Re: ai and their dumb hands
Pretty much
Today my eye swelled up for no reason and then we argued for ten minutes about which kind of pasta to cook so yeah that’s about right
They would and they have. For sure those kinds of guys have.
I had a bunch of sexy reference material that I used to make sexy artwork bht it got taken down so. Idk what people want frankly

item.dc:creator: softballguy

item.title: Thanks to Imgur, We're Getting Married
item.description:
Alrighty guys, let me give you a little life update with Imgur girl, @chewbacaisthehomie . It's been a while since my last post (I'm sorry!), but things have been going well I'd say. Quick recap: on Christmas Eve 2017, I was lurking around user subs, the place to find the best gold. As is tradition, I posted a Christmas selfie and so did this girl, @chewbacaisthehomie. I thought she was rather cute and noticed her Christmas salutation said seasons greetings from the same general area I live in. I attempted to use my boyish charm to slide into her DMs and try to further get to know her. Turns out we lived maybe 25 minutes from each other and had mutual friends! I somehow managed to have her agree to a date and we met in person 2 weeks later. Here we are 6 years later.
We discovered each other's passions, like food. We've eaten at super fancy restaurants together. We've now eaten at 11 Michelin starred restaurants! This is Alinea in Chicago.
We both like going to music festivals. Here's us dressed as Guy Fieri at Life is Beautiful in Las Vegas. We've gone to this festival 5 times now.
I taught her how to snowboard and now that is our shared hobby. We've boarded in 4 different countries now!
She fell in love with my cats and now they are inseperable.
We learned we both love to travel. Here is us on the plane heading to Denmark. We've been to 7 countries together!
She met my crazy European family in Estonia. This is our second time here together.
I figured let's seal the deal. Now we're getting married!!! We both love baseball so what better place to propose then on the field at Angel stadium, a place we've both grown up going to ball games as kids and have gone to dozens of games together now as a couple.
10-12-2024
So a big shoutout to @imgur. Without this platform for folks from all over the world to come together to share memes, laughs, tears, joy, empathy, and sometimes love, we would have never met. We hope you guys can come to the wedding next week! TL,DR: Met this chick on Imgur, dated for 6 years, and now we're getting married.

item.title: Cute - Two of Jack Black's 'School of Rock' Kids Are Engaged
item.description:
Marta and Frankie from School of Rock have got engaged in real life
Caitlin Hale, who played Marta – aka 'Blondie' – and Angelo Massagli, who played the role of Frankie – aka the 'tough guy' – first met on the set of the film when they were 11-years-old. Now, 18 years after the film's release, the pair are engaged to be married after dating for a number of years. In an Instagram post, Hale shares photos of Massagli and her – sporting an impressive engagement ring along with the caption "sneak peek". "We have a group chat where all the School of Rock kids, we just hit each other up whenever someone moves somewhere new or is doing something different. So Caitlin is like, hey, I'm moving down to Florida." Massagli lives in Florida so they met for lunch and romance blossomed. Since starring in the movie, the couple have gone on to have successful careers away from the limelight. Massagli is a lawyer and Hale is an ultrasound technologist and medical sonographer.
The wedding music should be rockin'
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.dc:creator: lydecker17

item.title: It's. Finally. DONE. The 1912 Neglect-o-Matic Restoration is DONE.
item.description:
Previously on Dr. Horse, Medicine Horse: https://imgur.com/gallery/3JaO5zy What an absolute mess of a machine! Aaaaugh. But it's done! And this is the post that tells you all about how it got done! Did I mention it's done? It's DONNNE. Blurgh. This was easily the most frustration project I've undertaken, and the most affirming in the sense that it forced me to revisit skills I'd only recently learned and apply them again with better end results. At the end of the day I can't call that a bad thing. Just that learning can hurt my brain some days. @.@ So, without further ado, let's talk about the last steps I took to put this slimy monster back together again.
First off, as you saw in the thumbnail, the price flags! As luck would have it, the parts that I feared were lost in the mail were in fact just not being scanned on their way through USPS. Freaking relief, that. Once the decals came in for the price flags I got to work slapping these bad boys on.
Of course, the "Paid Out" decal for the blue flag didn't get sent because I forgot to request it... so I had to make a quick order for that and wait until it showed up in my mailbox yesterday. Whoops!
Before I put the flags back into the frame, I needed to clean off the old grease and grime that was causing them to stick when I first bought it. No need for chemicals here, just a wipe down with a dry rag. Also, check out that cool font on the 26F stamp. Freaking love that font.
Bingo bango bongo, flags only took a little finessing to get everything arranged properly so nobody accidentally rammed into their neighbor. There's not a lot of space to work between each flag so scraping and scratching is a real possibility if anything gets bent. Lookin' good already!
Next came these bastards. I call them bastards because they are all little bastards. They're thin, they're plated, and they crack if you so much as look at them the wrong way. And of course because this register was clearly *dropped* at some point, they're all cracked to one degree or another. Ah well, as I've heard it said before, you piss with the cock you've got. Granted I don't have a, well... anyway.
Spent a half hour on the buffing wheel, this time in the garage with a mask on like smart people do who are aware of basic safety. Still working on a way to get my little garage set up so that I can actually do some of this out there without tripping over shovels and crap. Ah well. Shiny key rings!
No sense in jamming shiny rings onto filthy metal. Some oven cleaner makes quick work of all that old grime and grease without affecting the plating beneath. They still don't look perfect when they're dried off, but they'll be a far sight better than where they were.
Looking like a cash register again! I was tempted to ask for the 90 cent key to be remade since it's a bit tilted, but at this point it doesn't even rank on my to-do list. Considering my resource for these is an old man somewhere in New Jersey, I'm just glad only one of them was cattywompus. ;p Now onto the fun part: rebuilding the case!
The first panels to go back on are the two backers, the bottom of which was an absolute nightmare and source of much profanity because - no surprise here - the screw holes embedded in the panel didn't match up to the screw holes bored through the bottom frame. That's because this register was definitely dropped at some point, which ended up turning the square frame into a somewhat trapezoidal frame. Fun. I eventually got it bolted down after beating the thing with a rubber mallet. With that done, the rest of the panels came a lot easier. (Badum-tss)
Next was the printer box panel. When I took this machine apart, there was all of one screw holding this slab of brass in place. The rest of them were all AWOL, gone through the scary door or twilight zone. Who knows. Thankfully I have boxes of replacement fasteners in case this happens, and I nearly used half my damn supply on this machine!
The other panel went on nice and easy, and the screws actually threaded with no fuss this time. A sign that things are being pulled into alignment the more it comes together.
Next comes the cross-brace that keeps the side panels aligned and doubles as a display for all the register's serial information. It's probably the chunkiest piece of metal on this machine beside the cast iron core and could probably be used as a flippin' hammer if I got the mind to try it out. It's basically just brass bar stock made to look pretty.
Next comes the cover for all the counter wheels. Still haven't been able to find a Turn To Lock knob for that little post that sticks out near the center. They're out there on eBay, but there's no way in heck I'm spending $50 on a flippin' knob.
Hard to see, but the spacer comb goes in next. Starting to take shape, this stinky machine. :)
Not sure what the little rail is called that covers the ends of the comb, but the comb-end-rail-covering-bar gets screwed in with moderate cussing because those screws are hard as hell to get at without slipping.
Now comes the part I've had this project on hold for. The last time I bought glass, it ran me just shy of $90 after tax. This time I went to a different local glass cutter in town and they charged me a whopping $31 for the same feckin' glass! They just earned themselves a regular customer!
Before I can put the glass in, I need to screw in the frames that hold it. These were so thin and soft that I didn't dare try to hammer them straight. I've never seen these kinds of frames on a register before - usually the glass is held inside, not outside - so I wasn't going to chance cracking them with metal fatigue just so they'd look a little nicer.
On they go, and the glass fits perfectly! No wobbling from the endcaps either. It's all snug as a bug! Woohoo!
On goes the lid, which looks pretty heckin' good considering the mess of green goo it had become when I took it off.
I happened to have an extra lid counter in my toolbox, so on it goes with a couple new fasteners. After all, why not?
All that's left is to deal with the marble shelf. Initially I was expecting to have to rebreak it to clean up that crack, but after thinking about it I decided to just leave the repair as-is and do my best to use bristle brushes to scuff up the old epoxy they used. That way the new epoxy I planned to use to repair that chip had something to bond to.
Fun fact about epoxy and me: I've never used it before now. Bet you can't even tell. That's pro work right there! <_< We won't talk about just how much sanding I had to do to get rid of all that excess. Suffice to say the whole shelf got a good amount of sanding across a spectrum of grits until I was finally going over it with 1500 grit wet/dry paper. After I had all the excess epoxy smoothed out and the surface of the marble was smooth to the touch, I went over it with countertop sealant to close up all the little pores and get it down to a glassy texture.
You can see that my chip repair isn't perfect, but oh well. I like to think I'm giving the next person who owns it a wonderful opportunity to learn! Yeah, that's it! Anyhow, the marble came with that nice brass floral washer and an ugly steel bolt to secure it with. Looking at the washer, I can't tell if this is the intended shape of the bolt head or if something else was used, but what I do know is that I don't want to have that nasty black metal messing up all my fancy work.
So I went to the hardware store and spent two bucks on brass replacements. Nyehehehe!
(Yes, those are the old bolts, ignore them. I got my pictures out of order.) With the marble looking as good as the marble is going to look, the very last step is to put the drawer back in!
Bang! Nothing hangs up, everything slides smoothly, and that tells me the frame is back to some semblance of its original alignment before it was hucked down an open elevator shaft.
All that's left is to drag it out of the workshop and display it!
All in all, not too freaking bad! It's still missing a #5 lock and a knob for the door, but they'll have to wait until something shows up on fleabay or marketplace. All I want to do is look at the shiny metal and feel glad that this restoration is finally DONE.
With some direct sunlight on it, it doesn't look too bad! There's still obvious wear and tear, and that's to be expected for something that survived since it was made in 1912.
Apparently I don't know how to hold a camera upright. Ah well, I'm a happy camper right now. <3
Even the cover looks nice in the sunlight! So nice that Story had come to remind me that it's been zero point nine minutes since I last fed her. Kitty on a mission!
Since finishing this one up today, I made a point to send photos to the guy who originally sold it to me since this was a cash register for his grandmother's ice cream business. He hasn't gotten back to me yet, but I'm of the opinion that if he offers to buy it back from me then it's his to take. Maybe it'd fare better as an indoor register than it did a garage register. Or maybe he won't want it back at all, who knows!
Getting chilly out. Cash register has goosepimples.
Overall, an extremely challenging restoration but not an insurmountable one! This machine could have easily become a donor machine for all the damage it had sustained, but what's the point of going to the race if you give up at the starting gun? It didn't come out in perfect condition, sure, but it's functional again and something I'd be happy to display to visitors. :)
And of course, the obligatory chime of a very repaired little bell.
For reference, here's how it looked when I first dragged its slimy green bulk into the house.
And here's how it looks now! Looking pretty slick for 112 years old!
Thanks to everyone who patiently follow these restorations! Without your encouragement, I don't think I'd be doing nearly as much with this hobby as I have! As always, if you want to contribute to the restoration project fund, I'm always happy to accept a few dollars from the community to help with the cost of resurrecting these big brass monsters. ^_^ https://ko-fi.com/elbowdeepinahorse/ Now it's time to take a break and relax! Oofdah. <3
item.dc:creator: ElbowDeepInAHorse

item.title: Off-grid computer, radio, and WiFi network in a box for when the Internet goes dark
item.description:
Debian Linux x86 PC in a water-resistant box with a full-size wireless keyboard and touchpad, 1080p HDMI display with speakers, GPS for location and clock sync, integrated reading/work lamp, OpenWRT LTE/WiFi router and Ethernet switch, active cooling, and more.
Bootup sequence, with gratuitous floppy disks
Under the keyboard. It's a bit messy, but clockwise from top left: Noctua ventilation fan, PC power button, power switches and USB 3 port, exhaust vent, SDR antenna, 12V 7Ah LiFePO4 battery, RTL-SDR mounted with VHB tape on a Lenovo Thinkcentre Tiny desktop PC, Ethernet switch, GL.iNet LTE router. WiFi and LTE Antennas for the router are mounted behind the display in the top half. Some of the antennas are over a foot long and have to be mounted diagonally to fit!
Ethernet port to connect external devices to the box's network, plus two Anderson PowerPole connectors for charging (they're connected together to allow daisy-chaining power)
External waterproof USB port!
And the whole thing is ridiculously portable, considering the contents.
It has a full mirror of the amd64 Debian package archives, so i can install any software any time without Internet (and so can any Debian PC on the same network)
Offline Wikipedia, Project Gutenberg ebooks, iFixit repair guides, and more, running with Kiwix-serve
OpenStreetMap server, providing offline maps of the entire planet
SDR at work, receiving radio signals and playing them via the speakers in the screen.
Raw GPS data from the receiver above the screen (I took this picture in my basement, so no location fix)
Oh, and it's running a FreePBX phone server in a virtual machine, so phone calls can be made to and from anyone connected to the local network, even if there's no cell service. If the box has Internet, calls can be made to the outside world too, relayed via a VPN to a SIP trunk connected to the public telephone network.
item.dc:creator: skylarmt
